x4freetext: Data for 197Au(2-He-6,xn), where x=2-7 reported in
A secondary beam of 6He nuclei with energy E=18*A MeV
was obtained in the channel of the AKULINA facility
achromatic separator.
Au-197 was used (details were reported in F0837001)
Multilayer telescope technique.
The beam was passed through the first group of
detectors delta-E1 and delta-E2 to identify particles
and reduce their energy. In one session, at high beam
intensities, dE1 and dE2 were replaced by plastic
delta-E and Si plates of appropriate thickness.
Then the beam was passed through the system of
detectors of an active collimator. Si (H = 13.6 mm)
and MCP detectors were used in this experiment.
Finally, the beam was incident on a target of Au (H=10,
20 um), located in the center of a CsI(Tl) gamma-
spectrometer with 4-pi geometry.
A p-i-n detector (S = 20*20 mm2, H=1400 um) with a
system for identifying charged particles in the form
of a pulse was used as E detector. A fast plastic
scintillator E-detector equipped with a PEM
photomultiplier was used.
